coredata.dc:description: The rational design of efficient and stable photocatalysts has become an emerging research domain owing to its promising potential for solar fuel generation and environmental remediation. Among the different kinds of photocatalysts, Quantum dots (QDs) are acquiring considerable interest due to their unique properties, such as size-dependent bandgap, adjustable energy band levels, multi-exciton formation from single-photon absorption, and high surface area. Nevertheless, the existing challenges, including poor stability, wide bandgaps, and high recombination rate of photoinduced electron-hole pairs continue to hinder their photocatalytic efficiency. QDs-based S-scheme heterojunctions have emerged as high-profile photocatalysts, offering excellent light absorption and increased separation of high-energy photoinduced charge electron-hole pairs. The current review summarizes the photocatalytic properties of QDs, explores the basic concepts and mechanisms of S-scheme heterojunction, and discusses various QDs-based S-scheme photocatalysts, including low bandgap QDs, wide bandgap QDs, and graphene quantum dots (GQDs), for different photocatalytic applications. Moreover, it provides insights into future perspectives for designing effective QDs-based S-scheme photocatalysts. The current review aims to provide a useful resource for scientists in designing high-performance S-scheme heterojunction photocatalysts based on QDs and their composites.
